title: rhinanthus elatus
intro: Rhinanthus elatus, also known as the tall belvedere, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This plant is a hemiparasite, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. Rhinanthus elatus has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is believed to have potential health benefits including reducing stress, improving sleep, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared for use in herbal remedies through infusion, tincture, or decoction, with some herbalists recommending it as a treatment for anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
